svg.text: MERN Stack · Node.js / Express · React / React Router · MongoDB · PayPal
utils.js injects isAuth / isAdmin on protected routes
Two Routes share the same path. React Router v6 matches the first → OrderScreen runs unprotected. The ProtectedRoute wrapper is never reached.
Bug 2 — Duplicate element prop on /orderhistory and /profile
JSX silently ignores duplicate props; the ProtectedRoute element is discarded. Both screens are effectively public.
Declared in both index.js and App.js. The inner wrapper overrides the outer, making the one in index.js useless. Remove it from one file.
Bug 4 — Navbar links to /admin/orders (route does not exist)
App.js defines /admin/dashboard for DashboardScreen. The Navbar "Placed Orders" link points to /admin/orders → 404.
Bug 5 — Store.js: wrong localStorage key for userInfo
initialState reads localStorage.getItem('itemInfo') instead of 'userInfo' → userInfo is always null on page refresh.
The exported name is StorePorvider (not StoreProvider). index.js imports the typo correctly, but any future import risks confusion.
Bug 7 — ProfileScreen: submitHandler defined inside itself
The inner duplicate submitHandler + useReducer call inside the outer submitHandler is unreachable dead code (React rules of hooks violation).
Store.js reducer case CART_REMOVE_ITEM spreads state.card instead of state.cart. Remove works visually (cartItems is correct) but loses cart state.
All API calls originate from the React screens on the right and are handled by the Express routes on the left. Authentication is enforced server-side via utils.js (isAuth / isAdmin middleware).
The Store.js context (React useReducer) manages client-side state: cart items, userInfo, and checkout data, persisted to localStorage between sessions.
